definitions.text: An act of capturing; a seizing by force or stratagem.
The securing of an object of strife or desire, as by the power of some attraction.
examples: the capture of a lover’s heart

definitions.text: Something that has been captured; a captive.
To take control of; to seize by force or stratagem.
examples: to capture an enemy, a vessel, or a criminal
The paintings in the gallery really captured my imagination.

definitions.text: To store (as in sounds or image) for later revisitation.
examples: She captured the sounds of a subway station on tape.
She captured the details of the fresco in a series of photographs.
